_note: GENUINE news-derived seeds for BWET (Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF) — real events, real URLs. Catalyst: the 2026 Strait-of-Hormuz crisis. Iran blocked the strait on 2026-02-28 after US/Israeli strikes; the benchmark VLCC rate (Gulf->China, 2M bbl) hit an all-time high of ~$423,736/day on Mar-3 (+94% in a session) as insurers pulled war-risk cover and 150+ tankers anchored. BWET (~90% TD3C VLCC freight futures) became the best-performing ETF of 2026 (+785% YTD by Jul-3). ENTRY = the Mar-2026 rate-spike catalyst, framed as the still-under-owned tanker play (dialed-up under-owned framing per the RNMBY/MP method, since genuine BWET coverage names it only AFTER the surge). Replaces the prior synthetic BWET seeds. Look-ahead caveat: curator training likely postdates these dates (upper bound). Non-blank url = news-derived.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closure sends oil-tanker rates to record highs - Breakwave Tanker ETF (BWET) the under-owned way to play it
articles.snippet: With Iran blocking the Strait of Hormuz since Feb 28 after US-Israeli strikes, the benchmark VLCC rate to ship 2 million barrels of crude from the Gulf to China hit an all-time high of $423,736 per day - up more than 94% in a single session - as insurers dropped war-risk cover and 150-plus tankers anchored rather than transit. Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), about 90% VLCC (TD3C) freight futures, is the purest and still-under-owned way to own the spike as the disruption is only beginning.

articles.title: This little-known ETF is up over 600% amid U.S.-Iran war - a better trade than oil or energy stocks
articles.snippet: The Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET) has surged more than 600% year to date as the U.S.-Iran war and the Strait-of-Hormuz disruption drive tanker freight rates sharply higher. About 90% of the portfolio is TD3C futures - the cost of shipping crude on very large crude carriers from the Middle East to China - giving unlevered exposure to oil-tanker freight without a futures account. It is the best-performing ETF of 2026.
